text: The rapid rise of antibiotic-resistant bacteria poses a significant challenge to conventional wound care. In this study, we developed an innovative, antibiotic-free antimicrobial strategy utilizing hybrid nanozymes designed for enhanced wound healing. These hybrid nanozymes (FMA NPs) were engineered by facilitating the in-situ growth of ultra-small gold nanoparticles (Au NPs) onto metal-organic framework (MOF)-stabilized $Fe_3O_4$ nanoparticles. The resulting architecture exhibits synergistic peroxidase (POD)-like activity, generating high concentrations of lethal hydroxyl radicals (·OH) even at low, physiologically tolerable concentrations of $H_2O_2$ (0.97 mM). Our findings demonstrate that FMA nanozymes possess exceptional biocompatibility and exert potent antibacterial effects against both Gram-negative (Escherichia coli) and Gram-positive (Staphylococcus aureus) pathogens. In vivo experiments further confirmed that these nanozymes significantly accelerate wound repair while maintaining a favorable biosafety profile. This well-defined hybrid nanozyme platform represents a promising, non-toxic alternative for the treatment of bacterial infections, offering a robust tool for future antimicrobial therapies.
